  "summary": "This chunk contains detailed ritual instructions from the Munich Handbook of necromancy (CLM 849), as analyzed by Kieckhefer. It includes four major rituals: invisibility (No. 11) invoking Firiel, Melemil, Berith, and Taraor; love magic (No. 12) calling upon Belial, Astaroth, and Paymon; constraining spirits/humans/beasts (No. 13) using Asyel, Castiel, Lamisniel, Rabam, Erlain, and Belam; plus flying throne (No. 15), sleep divination (No. 16), and additional horse rituals (Nos. 14 & 17). Ritual ingredients include wax, red silk thread, needles, incense, holy water, and animal blood; tools include swords, spatulas, circles with directional markers, and diagrams. Many spirits are invoked via Trinitarian formulas and divine names like Tetragramaton.",
  "key_passages": [
    "Ego, talis, coniuro vos, O Fyriel, Mememil, Berith, [ et] Taraor, spiritus potentes, magnifici, et illustres,- in quibus omnino confido...",
    "Tu Belial, tu Astarothtf tu Paymon, ad hoc sitis opus michi adiutores.",
    "Asyel, Castiel, Lamisniel, Rabam, Erlain, [et\ Belamf vobis precipio... vt statim et cito et velociter et sine mora seducatis cor et mentem N. in amorem meum.",
    "O altissime et benignissime rex Orientis, exaudi oracionem meam et clamor meus ad te veniat...",
    "Vade ad locum secretum et altum, tempore sereno et absque ventu, et die: Sancta Maria, ora pro nobis..."
  ]
}
